title: checkForServerUpgrade fails for INSERT INTO.SELECT FOR UPDATE routines
description:
Hi,

When running checkForServerUpgrade for 57-->80 upgrade, checks will fail for routines which contain INSERT INTO .. SELECT FOR UPDATE with " unexpected token 'FOR' "

The same for just SELECT .. FOR UPDATE does not return an error.

Thanks,
Marc
How to repeat:
1. Create two simple procs. One with SELECT FOR UPDATE and another with INSERT INTO… SELECT…FOR UPDATE on a 5.7.43 database. 

create database if not exists test;
use test;
delimiter //
drop procedure if exists test_sel_for_uptd//
CREATE PROCEDURE `test_sel_for_uptd`()
BEGIN
     SELECT
           id,
           val
     FROM
           foo FOR UPDATE;
END//
delimiter ;
 
delimiter //
drop procedure if exists test_insert_into_sel_for_uptd//
CREATE PROCEDURE `test_insert_into_sel_for_uptd`()
BEGIN
     INSERT INTO test
           (id, val)
     SELECT
           id,
           val
     FROM
           foo FOR UPDATE;
END//
delimiter ;

2. Run checkForServerUpgrade which will return an error for "INSERT INTO… SELECT…FOR UPDATE." (test_insert_into_sel_for_uptd) but not SELECT FOR UPDATE (test_sel_for_uptd)

 MySQL  localhost:5743 ssl  JS > util.checkForServerUpgrade()
The MySQL server at localhost:5743, version 5.7.43 - MySQL Community Server
(GPL), will now be checked for compatibility issues for upgrade to MySQL
8.0.34...

1) Usage of old temporal type
  No issues found

2) MySQL 8.0 syntax check for routine-like objects
  The following objects did not pass a syntax check with the latest MySQL 8.0
    grammar. A common reason is that they reference names that conflict with new
    reserved keywords. You must update these routine definitions and `quote` any
    such references before upgrading.
  More information:
https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/keywords.html
test.test_insert_into_sel_for_uptd - at line 8,15: unexpected token 'FOR'

.
.
.

3. Ignore error and attempt upgrade. It will be successful.

Suggested fix:
Ensure INSERT INTO… SELECT…FOR UPDATE is correctly handled during routine parsing in routinesSyntaxCheck.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE foo (id INT PRIMARY KEY, val VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO foo (id, val) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2'), (3, NULL);
CREATE TABLE test (id INT, val VARCHAR(255), UNIQUE (id));
INSERT INTO test (id, val) SELECT id, val FROM foo WHERE val IS NOT NULL;
CREATE PROCEDURE test_insert_into_sel_for_uptd() BEGIN INSERT INTO test (id, val) SELECT id, val FROM foo;
key_bug_features: INSERT INTO SELECT FOR UPDATE routine syntax check failure
